stories.title: Medvedev Stunned by Australian Wildcard Walton — Biggest Upset of RG 2026

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: UPSET: Adam Walton, world No. 97 Australian wildcard, stuns No. 6 seed Medvedev 6-2, 1-6, 6-1, 1-6, 6-4 at Roland Garros. Biggest shock of the 2026 French Open. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #ATP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Medvedev's wife Daria to him during his Roland Garros loss: "Everybody's suffering — you need to behave."

His reply: "When I start finding the court I'll start to behave."

Day 3. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #ATP

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

The biggest upset of Roland Garros 2026 just happened — and it came with one of the best sideline moments of the season.

Australian wildcard Adam Walton, ranked No. 97 in the world, defeated No. 6 seed Daniil Medvedev 6-2, 1-6, 6-1, 1-6, 6-4 in 3 hours and 22 minutes on Court Suzanne-Lenglen. It was Walton's first career victory over a top-10 player, and the biggest result of his career. He said a win over Medvedev in Cincinnati last year gave him the confidence he could do it again in Paris.

But as dramatic as the match was, the moment that went viral was happening in the stands. During the contest, Medvedev engaged in a heated exchange with his wife Daria, who told him bluntly: "Everybody's suffering — you need to behave." Medvedev's reply? "When I start finding the court I'll start to behave." After the match, Medvedev was reflective: "I know I'm in good shape and I know I can play well at Roland Garros. It's just more difficult for me here and the early rounds tend to be tougher."

With Medvedev out, the draw opens up for players like Djokovic and the unseeded contenders still standing. Roland Garros loves its chaos.

Have you ever seen a bigger upset at Roland Garros?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

Roland Garros 2026's biggest upset: Australian wildcard Adam Walton (No. 97) stuns Daniil Medvedev (No. 6 seed) 6-2, 1-6, 6-1, 1-6, 6-4. Medvedev's Day 3 ends on the Lenglen — and so does his wife's patience.

article.headline: Australian Wildcard Adam Walton Stuns Medvedev in Roland Garros 2026's Biggest Upset

stories.title: Sinner Extends Win Streak to 30, Career Grand Slam Quest Rolls On

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Jannik Sinner def. Tabur 6-1, 6-3, 6-4 at Roland Garros. Win streak reaches 30 — 5th longest in ATP Tour history. 40 winners. 0 break points faced. The Career Grand Slam quest rolls on. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #ATP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Sinner: 30 wins in a row. Roland Garros is the only Grand Slam he hasn't won. He didn't face a single break point vs. Tabur. 40 winners in 2h08m. Next: Cerundolo. The mission continues. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #ATP

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Jannik Sinner is in a different dimension right now.

The world No. 1 defeated French wildcard Clément Tabur 6-1, 6-3, 6-4 on Tuesday night at Court Philippe-Chatrier, running his win streak to 30 consecutive matches. That's the fifth-longest streak in ATP Tour history since 1990. Over 2 hours and 8 minutes, Sinner hit 40 winners and — remarkably — did not face a single break point. He did miss three match points while closing out the final game, but ultimately won on his fifth attempt.

What makes this stretch even more staggering is the context: Sinner won five consecutive Masters 1000 titles leading into Roland Garros, something no player in the event's 36-year history had done before. Now he's chasing something even bigger — a Career Grand Slam. Roland Garros is the only major title he's still missing.

His next opponent is Francisco Cerundolo. The mission continues.

Do you think Sinner wins his first Roland Garros title in 2026?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Jannik Sinner def. Tabur 6-1, 6-3, 6-4 on Tuesday night. Thirty straight. Zero break points faced. One Slam still to win. Roland Garros 2026 is Sinner's biggest remaining mission.

article.headline: Sinner Extends Winning Streak to 30 with Dominant Night Session Win Over Tabur

stories.title: Gauff Opens Title Defense with Dominant Win; Osaka Dazzles in Gold

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Day 3 women at Roland Garros: Gauff def. Townsend 6-4, 6-0 — title defense rolling. Osaka def. Siegemund 6-3, 7-6 in gold sequins. Sabalenka already through. All three top seeds advancing. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #WTA

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Day 3 at Roland Garros delivered everything — results, drama, and one of the most talked-about outfits in recent tennis memory.

Defending champion Coco Gauff opened her title defense with a dominant 6-4, 6-0 win over American Taylor Townsend. Gauff was sharp and relentless, dropping just four games across two sets. She's looking every bit the champion who lifted the trophy here a year ago.

But it was Naomi Osaka who provided the Day 3 moment people will remember. Wearing a glittering gold sequined Nike dress — one she described as inspired by "the Eiffel Tower at night when it's sparkly" — Osaka defeated Germany's Laura Siegemund 6-3, 7-6(3) to advance to the second round. She joked that she worried the reflective dress might get her removed from the court: "When the sun hits the dress, it reflects a lot, so I was a little scared the umpire was going to kick me off the court."

No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka had already advanced in the morning session. All three stars through — in 35°C Paris heat.

Which Day 3 moment were you most excited about — Gauff's dominant win or Osaka's golden moment?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Gauff def. Townsend 6-4, 6-0. Osaka def. Siegemund 6-3, 7-6 in the gold dress of the year. Day 3 at Roland Garros had it all. All three top women's seeds are through.

article.headline: Gauff Opens Title Defense, Osaka Dazzles in Gold — Roland Garros Day 3 Women's Wrap

stories.title: Day 4 Preview — Swiatek, Djokovic, Zverev Night Session

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Day 4 at Roland Garros: Swiatek opens Chatrier vs. Bejlek. Djokovic vs. Royer midday. Rybakina on Lenglen. Zverev vs. Machac night session. Andreeva on Mathieu. Full day of tennis. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #ATP

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:30 PM ET

Day 4 at Roland Garros brings the marquee names out in full force — and after Medvedev's shock Day 3 exit, the stakes feel even higher.

Court Philippe-Chatrier opens with four-time champion Iga Swiatek against Czech Sara Bejlek. Then three-time champion Novak Djokovic steps in against Frenchman Valentin Royer in a match the home crowd will have a strong opinion about. The night session belongs to No. 2 seed Alexander Zverev against Tomas Machac, one of the most tactically interesting matches on the card.

Court Suzanne-Lenglen features No. 2 Elena Rybakina against Yuliia Starodubtseva, 2024 finalist Jasmine Paolini, and two-time finalist Casper Ruud all in action. No. 8 seed Mirra Andreeva leads a loaded program on Court Simonne-Mathieu alongside Belinda Bencic and an all-French matchup between Ugo Humbert and Quentin Halys.

With Medvedev already gone, the draw is wide open. Who navigates through Day 4?

Who are you most looking forward to watching on Day 4?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 12:30 PM ET

Day 4 at Roland Garros: Swiatek opens Chatrier. Djokovic vs. Royer midday. Zverev vs. Machac at night. Rybakina and Paolini on Lenglen. It's a big one.

article.headline: Roland Garros Day 4 Preview: Djokovic, Swiatek, Rybakina, Zverev Night Session

stories.title: Osaka's Eiffel Tower Moment — The Story Behind the Gold Dress

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Naomi Osaka on her gold Roland Garros dress: "You know the Eiffel Tower at night when it's sparkly? I think I look like that a little bit." She won 6-3, 7-6. Icon. #RolandGarros #FrenchOpen #Tennis #WTA

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

There are tennis moments and then there are Naomi Osaka moments — and on Day 3 at Roland Garros, she delivered both.

Osaka walked onto Court Suzanne-Lenglen wearing a black corset and pleated skirt designed by Kevin Germanier. Then she pulled it back to reveal what was underneath: a glittering, gold-sequined Nike dress. As she explained afterward, she had a specific inspiration in mind: "You know the Eiffel Tower at night when it's sparkly? I think I look like that a little bit."

She also had a very practical concern. "When the sun hits the dress, it reflects a lot, so I was a little scared the umpire was going to kick me off the court." No such intervention was required — Osaka went on to beat Germany's Laura Siegemund 6-3, 7-6(3) to advance to the second round.

Even No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka commented on the outfit. Osaka's comeback from a difficult few years is well documented — she was ranked No. 135 at the start of 2025 — and this Roland Garros run is another chapter in that story.

What do you think of Osaka's gold sequin look?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

Naomi Osaka at Roland Garros 2026: "You know the Eiffel Tower at night when it's sparkly? I think I look like that a little bit." She won too. 6-3, 7-6. On to the second round.

article.headline: Osaka's Eiffel Tower Dress Steals Day 3 at Roland Garros 2026
